title: ODI Matrix — Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511)

sections.title: Step 1 — Error Statement Collection
sections.content:
25 needs from Step 06 (JTBD_finfish_farming_and_fish_hatcheries) + 5 product-related needs from Step 10 (FIT_finfish_farming_and_fish_hatcheries) = **30 total needs**.

Step 10 entries injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"`, `product_related=true` per merge rule.

No markets skipped — 30 needs found for Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries.

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration (Incumbent-Grounded)
sections.content:
**Incumbent profiles used for recalibration:**

| Incumbent | Share | Mechanism | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Paddle-wheel / turbine | 45–55% | Rotational (impeller) | Lowest cost ($20–80), simple installation, no fluid config needed, flow-profile-insensitive | Clogs with biofilm every 2–6 weeks in RAS, ±2–5% drift, no diagnostics, moving parts wear |
| Electromagnetic (mag) | 15–25% | Faraday induction | ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, 100:1 turndown, no air/temperature sensitivity | 5–10× cost ($500–2,000+), 10–20 W power, larger footprint, requires conductive liquid |
| Do nothing / manual | 15–25% | Visual / behavioral observation | Zero cost | Blind to flow failure; relies on fish behavior or downstream DO alarm; catastrophic in RAS |

[SRC: JTBD_finfish_farming §2.4 Alternatives, VN_finfish_farming]

### Recalibration Table — Step 06 Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Recalibration Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Flow cessation detection <60s | 10 | 3 | **2** | PW (dominant) + manual (15–25%) provide near-zero real-time per-tank cessation alarm; PW requires SCADA integration most mid-scale farms lack |
| 2 | Calibration drift <±1% / 12mo | 8 | 3 | **3** | PW drifts 2–5% in biofilm water (dominant); mag stable but minority share |
| 3 | Temperature accuracy 2–30°C | 7 | 4 | **5** | Both PW and mag measurement principles are temperature-insensitive for flow reading |
| 4 | Full range 2–60 L/min | 7 | 5 | **5** | Mag excellent turndown (100:1); PW limited at low flow — mixed |
| 5 | Pump degradation detection >20% | 10 | 2 | **2** | No incumbent trends pump degradation; manual checks only |
| 6 | Pipe blockage detection >15% | 9 | 3 | **3** | PW detects gross blockage but not subtle 15% restriction; biofilm on PW itself confounds reading |
| 7 | Anomaly classification >95% | 8 | 3 | **2** | No incumbent provides anomaly classification; PW has zero diagnostics; mag has no classification logic |
| 8 | False flow-normal during air-lock | 9 | 3 | **3** | PW stalls obviously during complete air-lock; partial air-lock masking remains a concern across technologies |
| 9 | Flow rate setpoint ±10% of O2 demand | 8 | 3 | **3** | Knowledge/engineering need; sensor-independent |
| 10 | Per-tank flow calculation effort | 6 | 4 | **4** | Knowledge need; sensor-independent |
| 11 | Install without draining circuit | 8 | 3 | **3** | All inline sensors require circuit drain for installation |
| 12 | Medium config error >1% | 7 | 4 | **6** | PW (dominant) needs zero fluid configuration — zero config error risk; mag needs minimal config |
| 13 | Verify accuracy in 10 min pre-stocking | 8 | 3 | **3** | PW has no self-diagnostics; pre-stocking verification mostly skipped in practice |
| 14 | False-normal after maintenance | 9 | 3 | **2** | No incumbent has post-maintenance verification logic; unaddressed across all technologies |
| 15 | Isolate root cause in 3 min | 9 | 2 | **2** | No incumbent provides diagnostic data for root cause isolation |
| 16 | Operator expertise for alarm data | 8 | 3 | **3** | PW: no diagnostics; mag: complex outputs requiring training |
| 17 | Service life >5yr zero clogging | 9 | 2 | **2** | PW clogs every 2–6 weeks (catastrophic); dominant incumbent is worst-case for this need |
| 18 | Installed cost <$400/point | 8 | 4 | **5** | PW at $20–80 well under $400 target; mag at $500–2,000 exceeds it |
| 19 | No flow path contamination | 7 | 5 | **5** | PW: some debris risk from bearing lubricant; mag: clean — mixed |
| 20 | Pressure drop minimization | 7 | 5 | **5** | PW and mag both have minimal pressure drop |
| 21 | Total stock loss from circulation failure | 10 | 3 | **2** | Dominant incumbents (PW + manual = 60–80%) provide inadequate protection against complete stock loss |
| 22 | Water exchange ±10% all tanks | 8 | 4 | **4** | PW too imprecise for per-tank balancing; mag accurate but expensive per-point |
| 23 | Quantitative performance data | 7 | 3 | **3** | PW: basic pulse only; mag: good data but too expensive for full deployment |
| 24 | Overnight mass mortality detection | 10 | 3 | **2** | PW + manual have no reliable overnight alarming at per-tank level |
| 25 | Anxiety leaving facility unattended | 8 | 3 | **3** | Emotional need; PW lacks reliable remote alerting capability |

### Recalibration Table — Step 10 Product-Related Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Recalibration Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-1 | Air >2% measurement overestimation | 7 | 4 | **4** | PW jams entirely with air (worse but obvious failure); mag handles air better — mixed |
| PRN-2 | Pipe partially-filled blackout | 6 | 4 | **4** | PW also loses measurement with partially filled pipe; mag has empty-pipe detection |
| PRN-3 | Short straight pipe accuracy | 6 | 4 | **5** | PW is flow-profile-insensitive; mag needs shorter straight run (≤5×DN vs 5–10×DN for ultrasonic) |
| PRN-4 | Rapid temperature transient error | 5 | 6 | **7** | Neither PW nor mag has temperature-dependent flow measurement error; purely ultrasonic concern |
| PRN-5 | Below minimum detection threshold | 6 | 5 | **6** | PW detects >0.5 l/min; mag detects >0.2 l/min at DN20; both better than ultrasonic DN20 minimum of 4.2 l/min |

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity Scores (Deterministic)
sections.content:
Formula: `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
Underserved: `importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
Overserved: `importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Step 06 Needs — Scored

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under? | Over? | Impact | prod_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Flow cessation detection <60s | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | YES | no | safety | false |
| 2 | Calibration drift <±1% / 12mo | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 3 | Temperature accuracy 2–30°C | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | no | no | reliability | false |
| 4 | Full range 2–60 L/min | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| 5 | Pump degradation detection >20% | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | YES | no | safety | false |
| 6 | Pipe blockage detection >15% | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 7 | Anomaly classification >95% | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 8 | False flow-normal during air-lock | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | YES | no | safety | true |
| 9 | Flow rate setpoint ±10% of O2 demand | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | safety | false |
| 10 | Per-tank flow calculation effort | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | no | no | skill | false |

| 17 | Service life >5yr zero clogging | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | YES | no | cost | true |
| 18 | Installed cost <$400/point | 8 | 5 | **11.00** | no | no | cost | true |

### Step 10 Product-Related Needs — Scored

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRN-1 | Air >2% measurement overestimation | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| PRN-2 | Pipe partially-filled blackout | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| PRN-3 | Short straight pipe accuracy | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| PRN-4 | Rapid temperature transient error | 5 | 7 | **5.00** | no | no | reliability | true |

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Finfish Farming and Fish Hatcheries (NAICS 112511) |
| **Total Needs** | 30 |
| **Underserved Count** | 17 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 12.13 |

### Top 5 Opportunities

| Rank | # | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1 | Minimize the likelihood of undetected flow cessation in a rearing tank supply line persisting longer than 60 seconds before alarm activation | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 2 | 5 | Minimize the time to detect a circulation pump degradation event that reduces tank water exchange rate by >20% before dissolved oxygen drops below 6 mg/L | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 3 | 21 | Minimize the likelihood of a total stock loss event (>10% biomass mortality) caused by undetected water circulation failure in any single rearing unit | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 4 | 24 | Minimize the likelihood of discovering a mass mortality event caused by overnight flow failure that was not detected by the monitoring system | 10 | 2 | **18.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 5 | 14 | Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading after system maintenance that masks a partially closed valve or misrouted pipe | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | Highly Underserved |

**Runners-up at Opp=16:** #15 (isolate anomaly root cause in 3 min), #17 (service life >5yr zero clogging).

### Opportunity Landscape

| Zone | Score Range | Count | % of Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved | > 15 | 7 | 23.3% |
| Underserved | 12–15 | 10 | 33.3% |
| Appropriately Served | 10–12 | 3 | 10.0% |
| Overserved | < 10 | 10 | 33.3% |

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check vs Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg_opportunity_score | 12.13 | This analysis |
| Feature-Fit avg (tech features) | 5.18 | FIT_finfish_farming §09a (mean of 7 features) |

**Cross-check thresholds:**

| Condition | Triggered? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg > 12 AND Feature-Fit avg < 4 | **NO** | Feature-Fit avg = 5.18 (above 4) |
| ODI avg < 8 AND Feature-Fit avg > 7 | **NO** | ODI avg = 12.13 (above 8) |

**No inconsistencies detected.** ODI shows extensive unmet needs (17 underserved, 7 highly underserved) while Feature-Fit shows moderate-to-good technology relevance (5.18 avg). The top opportunities (#1, #5, #21, #24 — all at Opp=18) are life-safety needs that align with the sensor's highest-scoring features: Digital Anomaly Detection (fit=6.67) and No Moving Parts (fit=6.89). The market's dominant incumbent (paddle-wheel, 45–55%) is catastrophically poor at the highest-value needs (clogging destroys reliability, no diagnostics for detection), creating a massive opportunity gap for ultrasonic technology.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | **PASS** | Spot-checked 5 entries: #1 (10+max(10-2,0)=18), #12 (7+max(7-6,0)=8), #17 (9+max(9-2,0)=16), PRN-4 (5+max(5-7,0)=5), PRN-3 (6+max(6-5,0)=7). All match. |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | **PASS** | 17 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5; verified #1(10,2)=YES, #3(7,5)=NO (7 not >7), #18(8,5)=NO (5 not <5), #22(8,4)=YES |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | **PASS** | 0 entries with imp<3 AND sat>7; no need has importance <3 |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | **PASS** | 25 (Step 06) + 5 (Step 10) = 30 ≥ 25 (Step 06 count) |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | **PASS** | 18 > 18 > 18 > 18 > 16 — descending by opp score; ties broken by need number |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | **PASS** | ODI vs Feature-Fit comparison completed; no inconsistencies |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- JTBD_finfish_farming_and_fish_hatcheries.md (25 error statements, incumbent anchors, alternatives, ODI pre-scores)
- FIT_finfish_farming_and_fish_hatcheries.md (5 product-related needs from Step 10, 7 tech feature-market fit scores from Step 09)
- VN_finfish_farming_and_fish_hatcheries.md (incumbent technology profiles, market context)
- 01_product_decomposition.md (constraints generating product-related needs)
- 02_functional_promise.md (commodity FP baseline)
- 05_market_discovery.md (NAICS 112511 — market context, alternatives)

rows.Strengths: Lowest cost ($20–80), simple installation, no fluid config needed, flow-profile-insensitive
rows.Weaknesses: Clogs with biofilm every 2–6 weeks in RAS, ±2–5% drift, no diagnostics, moving parts wear

rows.Strengths: ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, 100:1 turndown, no air/temperature sensitivity
rows.Weaknesses: 5–10× cost ($500–2,000+), 10–20 W power, larger footprint, requires conductive liquid
Blind to flow failure; relies on fish behavior or downstream DO alarm; catastrophic in RAS

rows.Recalibration Rationale: PW (dominant) + manual (15–25%) provide near-zero real-time per-tank cessation alarm; PW requires SCADA integration most mid-scale farms lack
PW drifts 2–5% in biofilm water (dominant); mag stable but minority share
Both PW and mag measurement principles are temperature-insensitive for flow reading
Mag excellent turndown (100:1); PW limited at low flow — mixed
No incumbent trends pump degradation; manual checks only
PW detects gross blockage but not subtle 15% restriction; biofilm on PW itself confounds reading
No incumbent provides anomaly classification; PW has zero diagnostics; mag has no classification logic
PW stalls obviously during complete air-lock; partial air-lock masking remains a concern across technologies

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Flow rate setpoint ±10% of O2 demand
rows.Recalibration Rationale: Knowledge/engineering need; sensor-independent
Knowledge need; sensor-independent
All inline sensors require circuit drain for installation
PW (dominant) needs zero fluid configuration — zero config error risk; mag needs minimal config
PW has no self-diagnostics; pre-stocking verification mostly skipped in practice
No incumbent has post-maintenance verification logic; unaddressed across all technologies
No incumbent provides diagnostic data for root cause isolation
PW: no diagnostics; mag: complex outputs requiring training
PW clogs every 2–6 weeks (catastrophic); dominant incumbent is worst-case for this need
PW at $20–80 well under $400 target; mag at $500–2,000 exceeds it
PW: some debris risk from bearing lubricant; mag: clean — mixed
PW and mag both have minimal pressure drop

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Total stock loss from circulation failure
rows.Recalibration Rationale: Dominant incumbents (PW + manual = 60–80%) provide inadequate protection against complete stock loss
PW too imprecise for per-tank balancing; mag accurate but expensive per-point
PW: basic pulse only; mag: good data but too expensive for full deployment
PW + manual have no reliable overnight alarming at per-tank level
Emotional need; PW lacks reliable remote alerting capability
PW jams entirely with air (worse but obvious failure); mag handles air better — mixed
PW also loses measurement with partially filled pipe; mag has empty-pipe detection
PW is flow-profile-insensitive; mag needs shorter straight run (≤5×DN vs 5–10×DN for ultrasonic)
Neither PW nor mag has temperature-dependent flow measurement error; purely ultrasonic concern
PW detects >0.5 l/min; mag detects >0.2 l/min at DN20; both better than ultrasonic DN20 minimum of 4.2 l/min

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Flow rate setpoint ±10% of O2 demand
Total stock loss from circulation failure

rows.Error Statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected flow cessation in a rearing tank supply line persisting longer than 60 seconds before alarm activation
Minimize the time to detect a circulation pump degradation event that reduces tank water exchange rate by >20% before dissolved oxygen drops below 6 mg/L
Minimize the likelihood of a total stock loss event (>10% biomass mortality) caused by undetected water circulation failure in any single rearing unit
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a mass mortality event caused by overnight flow failure that was not detected by the monitoring system
Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading after system maintenance that masks a partially closed valve or misrouted pipe

rows.Note: 17 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5; verified #1(10,2)=YES, #3(7,5)=NO (7 not >7), #18(8,5)=NO (5 not <5), #22(8,4)=YES
0 entries with imp<3 AND sat>7; no need has importance <3
18 > 18 > 18 > 18 > 16 — descending by opp score; ties broken by need number
ODI vs Feature-Fit comparison completed; no inconsistencies

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected flow cessation in a rearing tank supply line persisting longer than 60 seconds before alarm activation
Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±1% of reading over a 12-month continuous operation period in biofilm-prone water
Minimize variation in flow measurement accuracy caused by seasonal water temperature fluctuations between 2°C and 30°C across species programs
Increase the ability to measure flow rate across the full operating range (2–60 L/min per tank feed) without sensor reconfiguration between tank sizes
Minimize the time to detect a circulation pump degradation event that reduces tank water exchange rate by >20% before dissolved oxygen drops below 6 mg/L
Minimize the number of undetected pipe blockage events (biofilm growth, scale accumulation) that restrict flow by >15% in treatment loops
Increase the ability to distinguish genuine flow anomalies (pump cavitation, valve failure) from sensor measurement artifacts with >95% classification accuracy
Minimize the likelihood of false flow-normal readings during air-lock events in recirculation piping that mask actual flow cessation
Increase the ability to determine the correct flow rate setpoint within ±10% of actual biological oxygen demand for each species, stocking density, and growth stage
Minimize the effort required to calculate per-tank flow requirements when stocking density changes during grow-out cycles
Minimize the time required to install and commission a flow sensor on an existing RAS pipe loop without draining the circuit or interrupting fish production
Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement error >1% caused by incorrect medium configuration at commissioning for recirculated water with varying dissolved solids
Increase the ability to verify sensor accuracy on-site within 10 minutes before stocking fish into a newly prepared tank
Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading after system maintenance that masks a partially closed valve or misrouted pipe
Increase the ability to isolate flow anomaly root cause (sensor fault vs pump failure vs valve position vs pipe blockage) within 3 minutes during an active alarm
Minimize the operator expertise required to interpret flow alarm data and determine the correct corrective action during a night shift with minimal staffing
Increase the sensor service life achievable without scheduled maintenance interventions in biofilm-prone recirculated water (target: >5 years with zero clogging events)
Minimize the total installed cost per water flow measurement point including sensor, wiring, and integration with existing PLC/SCADA (target: <$400)
Minimize the likelihood of sensor-induced flow path contamination or turbulence that affects dissolved oxygen transfer downstream of the measurement point
Minimize the permanent pressure drop across the measurement point that increases circulation pump energy consumption in an energy-intensive RAS facility
Minimize the likelihood of a total stock loss event (>10% biomass mortality) caused by undetected water circulation failure in any single rearing unit
Increase the ability to maintain water exchange rate within ±10% of setpoint across all rearing tanks simultaneously during peak biomass loading
Increase the ability to present quantitative water circulation performance data to facility investors, insurers, and certification auditors as evidence of operational control
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a mass mortality event caused by overnight flow failure that was not detected by the monitoring system
Minimize the anxiety associated with leaving a high-value RAS facility ($500K+ biomass at risk) unattended during off-hours by providing reliable remote flow-failure alerts
Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement overestimation caused by air entrainment exceeding 2% by volume in recirculation piping during oxygenation injection or post-maintenance air purging
Minimize the duration of total measurement blackout during pipe partially-filled states that occur during RAS circuit re-filling, pump restart sequences, or gravity-drain maintenance events
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation caused by turbulent flow profiles from elbows, valves, and tee fittings within 5–10 pipe diameters upstream of the sensor in compact RAS pipe layouts
Minimize the residual flow measurement error during rapid water temperature changes exceeding 5°C/hour that outpace the speed-of-sound compensation algorithm response rate
Minimize the probability of undetected flow reduction events where actual flow rate drops below the sensor minimum detection threshold in small-diameter nursery and fry-rearing feed lines

key.error_statement: Increase the sensor service life achievable without scheduled maintenance interve

properties.full_statement: Increase the sensor service life achievable without scheduled maintenance interventions in biofilm-prone recirculated water (target: >5 years with zero clogging events)

properties.full_statement: Increase the ability to present quantitative water circulation performance data to facility investors, insurers, and certification auditors as evidence of operational control

key.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a mass mortality event caused by overnight

properties.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a mass mortality event caused by overnight flow failure that was not detected by the monitoring system

properties.full_statement: Minimize the anxiety associated with leaving a high-value RAS facility ($500K+ biomass at risk) unattended during off-hours by providing reliable remote flow-failure alerts
